statement: The system shall provide proper budgeting functionality.
The system shall allow each team to manage its own budget while restricting the IFA to view‑only access for monitoring balances.
The system shall allow specification of budget policies and alert violations.
The system shall allow the financial team to access financial data of all teams.
The system shall allow administrative users to view diverse transactions.
The system shall allow referees to insert game events in real time using a mobile device.
The system shall make game result data publicly viewable to all users, including unregistered guests.
The system shall allow users to export data for journalists or press.
The system shall allow team managers and team administration to view data and perform offline analysis.
The system shall allow only teams to insert budget transactions and shall restrict each team to accessing only its own data.
The system shall allow the IFA to view all monetary information, receive alerts, and request details, but shall prevent the IFA from modifying the data.
The system shall allow definition of scheduling policies for games.
The system shall allocate referees to games while ensuring that no referee is assigned to two games involving the same teams.
The system shall enable referees to set their availability preferences and incorporate them into scheduling.
The system shall allow manual adjustment of the schedule.
The system shall require a rationale explanation and approval by a second IFA representative for any schedule override.
The system shall require that any schedule change be approved by two IFA representatives before it is applied.
The system shall enforce a minimum gap of two days between matches for the same teams.
The system shall allow definition of parameters such as minimum gap days based on the number of teams.
The system shall allow users to define scheduling parameters.
The system shall send push notifications to fans about subscribed games.
The system shall allow fans to subscribe to their favourite players and receive notifications when those players post a message.
The system shall provide team and player pages that fans can follow and comment on.
The system shall include sections for following teams and players that provide notifications, live referee updates, and live scores.
The system shall provide a web‑based application for teams and IFA administration and mobile applications for referees and fans.
The system shall provide a unified platform with distinct interfaces tailored to each stakeholder role.
The system shall allow only official referees to update game data.
The system shall send automatic reminder notifications, such as five minutes before a game starts.
The system shall support up to 50,000 concurrent users.
The system shall provide a response time of one to two seconds for most users and prioritize real-time performance for referees.
The system shall encrypt budget data to prevent leakage.
The system shall support registration of leagues, seasons, referees, and fans as basic functionality.
The system shall provide user profiles with usernames for all users.
The system shall provide a team registration workflow in which the IFA approves the request before the team can manage its players, coaches, stadium, and resources.
The system shall retain all past scheduling archives.
The system shall allow the IFA to define leagues and seasons, appoint and register referees, and set budget and scheduling policies.
The system shall allow the IFA to appoint referees and invite them to join the system.
